textPlain: > wg := sync.WaitGroup{}just `var wg sync.WaitGroup`, it is cleaner this way
This isn’t necessary anymore as of Go 1.22https://go.dev/blog/loopvar-preview

You can just use golang.org/x/sync/errgroup instead, which has always provided this style of use.errgroup also has other niceties like error propagation, context cancellation, and concurrency limiting.
You can wrap WaitGroup if you really want to.
doesn't this point to a bigger problem that there are two ways of doing the same thing?
